examples.title: Two-Phase Commit Coordinator Algorithm
examples.description: Pseudocode showing coordinator's role in 2PC protocol
examples.code:
COORDINATOR_ALGORITHM
BEGIN
 // Phase 1: Vote Phase
 SEND VOTE-REQUEST to all participants
 WAIT for responses from all participants
 
 // Check all votes
 allVotedCommit = TRUE
 FOR each response IN responses
 IF response == VOTE-ABORT THEN
 allVotedCommit = FALSE
 BREAK
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 // Phase 2: Decision Phase
 IF allVotedCommit THEN
 SEND GLOBAL-COMMIT to all participants
 ELSE
 SEND GLOBAL-ABORT to all participants
 END IF
 
 WAIT for ACK from all participants
 MARK transaction as COMPLETED
END
examples.explanation: Coordinator initiates 2PC by sending vote requests, collects all participant votes, makes global decision (commit if all vote commit, otherwise abort), and communicates final decision to all participants.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n = number of participants

examples.title: Two-Phase Commit Participant Algorithm
examples.description: Pseudocode showing participant's role in 2PC protocol
examples.code:
PARTICIPANT_ALGORITHM
BEGIN
 RECEIVE VOTE-REQUEST from coordinator
 
 // Execute transaction locally
 EXECUTE transaction operations
 WRITE undo/redo logs to stable storage
 
 // Decide vote
 IF transaction executed successfully THEN
 SEND VOTE-COMMIT to coordinator
 ELSE
 SEND VOTE-ABORT to coordinator
 END IF
 
 // Wait for global decision
 SET timeout timer
 WHILE no global decision received AND not timed out
 WAIT
 END WHILE
 
 IF received GLOBAL-COMMIT THEN
 COMMIT transaction using redo logs
 RELEASE all locks
 ELSE IF received GLOBAL-ABORT THEN
 ROLLBACK transaction using undo logs
 RELEASE all locks
 ELSE // timeout
 BLOCK - cannot proceed without coordinator decision
 END IF
END
examples.explanation: Participant executes transaction, writes logs, votes commit or abort, then waits for coordinator's global decision. If timeout occurs before decision, participant blocks - this is the blocking problem.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for logging and commit operations

examples.title: Three-Phase Commit Protocol
examples.explanation: 3PC adds an extra phase (PreCommit) to ensure participants know the decision before actual commit. If coordinator fails in PreCommit or later phases, participants can communicate and determine the correct action without blocking.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) - more rounds than 2PC but non-blocking
